title: Fix P-002: session-holders identity resolution for check_lock.py
description: check_lock.py only reads ambient CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID, so in-session subagents with per-call identity overrides self-block on their own locks (audit_gen1 Proposal A / P-002). Build .harness/bin/session.py (register/unregister/list session holders with TTL entries, guarded writes to .harness/session_holders.json — coordinator-owned, NEVER auto-registered by claims, so cross-engine/cross-session locks stay mechanically enforced). Add harness_common.session_holders() reading live entries. check_lock.py allows a write when lock holder == me OR holder in session_holders(); stays fail-open. Document semantics in .harness/README.md §Identity/§Locks (README is locked by fable-5-coordinator early on — do your code first, README last).
acceptance_criteria: synthetic PreToolUse payload with a lock held by a holder REGISTERED in session_holders returns exit 0; same payload with an UNREGISTERED holder (e.g. gemini-runner) still returns exit 2
session.py register/unregister/list work; entries carry TTL and expire automatically (prove with a --ttl 1 entry)
check_lock.py remains fail-open: malformed/empty stdin exits 0; py_compile clean on all touched files
README §Identity documents the mechanism + its cross-session semantics (registered = this session's coordinated agents ONLY)

notes.note: plan: add session.py (register/unregister/list, TTL-guarded JSON), harness_common.session_holders(), check_lock.py allows holder==me OR holder in session_holders(); README Identity section last after coordinator releases it

HANDOFF -> verifier: P-002 fixed. Files touched: .harness/bin/session.py (new: register <name> [--ttl 7200 --by <agent>] | unregister <name> | list; data file .harness/session_holders.json {"holders":{"<name>":{"registered_at","expires_at","by"}}}, all mutations under hc.guarded()+hc.atomic_write_json, expired entries physically pruned on every register/unregister write, list is read-only and filters live entries for display, events session_holder_registered/unregistered logged via hc.log_event); .harness/bin/harness_common.py (added SESSION_HOLDERS path const + session_holders() -> set of live names, wrapped in try/except returning set() on any error, no existing signatures changed); .harness/bin/check_lock.py (allow when holder==me OR holder in hc.session_holders(); docstring documents cross-session semantics; fail-open unchanged); .harness/README.md (SS Identity extended with the session-holder mechanism + explicit "registered = this session's coordinated agents ONLY, never auto-registered by claim/acquire" rule; directory map lists session.py and updated check_lock.py/harness_common.py one-liners).

REPLAY (a) TTL expiry, live-then-pruned: `python3 .harness/bin/session.py register probe-holder --ttl 1 && python3 .harness/bin/session.py list` -> immediately showed "no live session holders" (observed cause: harness_common.ISO_FMT truncates to whole seconds, so a --ttl 1 window can already be consumed by sub-second truncation + process-fork overhead by the time `list` runs in a fresh process -- pre-existing quirk in the shared time helpers, not introduced by this change, out of scope to fix here). For an unambiguous two-phase proof also ran: `register probe-holder-3s --ttl 3` -> `list` immediately showed it live (registered_at/expires_at populated); `sleep 4` -> `list` -> "no live session holders". `unregister probe-holder` (already-expired) then confirmed the file itself was rewritten to {"holders":{}} (physical prune-on-write verified by reading .harness/session_holders.json before/after).

REPLAY (b) core P-002 fix, no env override: `python3 .harness/bin/session.py register substrate-worker-2 --ttl 300` then `python3 .harness/bin/lock.py acquire .harness/logs/scratch_T008_probe.txt --holder substrate-worker-2 --task T-008` (scratch file created+deleted inside workspace root, since lock_name_for refuses paths outside ROOT). In a FRESH bash invocation with CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID unset (confirmed via `hc.agent_id()` -> "main"): `printf '%s' '{"tool_name":"Edit","tool_input":{"file_path":".harness/logs/scratch_T008_probe.txt"}}' | python3 .harness/bin/check_lock.py; echo $?` -> EXIT 0 (registered holder allowed). Then `python3 .harness/bin/session.py unregister substrate-worker-2` and repeated the identical piped payload -> EXIT 2 with "HARNESS LOCK: ... held by agent 'substrate-worker-2' ..." on stderr. Also cycled the lock to holder 'gemini-runner' (never registered, matches the acceptance-criteria example) -> same piped payload -> EXIT 2 as expected. Cleaned up: released scratch lock, deleted scratch file.

REPLAY (c) fail-open: `printf '' | python3 .harness/bin/check_lock.py; echo $?` -> 0. Also tested malformed non-JSON stdin (`printf 'not json {{{' | ...`) -> 0.

REPLAY (d) py_compile: `python3 -m py_compile .harness/bin/session.py .harness/bin/check_lock.py .harness/bin/harness_common.py` -> clean, no output, exit 0 (re-ran after all edits, confirmed clean again).

REPLAY (e) harness_common still works: `python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py status` -> ran cleanly, full board printed, no exceptions.

Final state left for the rest of this session per coordinator instruction: `python3 .harness/bin/session.py list` shows exactly substrate-worker-1 and substrate-worker-2 registered with ttl=7200 (expires ~2026-07-04T06:30:14Z), by=substrate-worker-1; no other probe holders remain (registry was confirmed {"holders":{}} immediately before this final pair of registrations). All locks I held (session.py, harness_common.py, check_lock.py, README.md) are released; `lock.py status` -> "no active lock files".

Design notes for the verifier to double check independently: (1) `list` is deliberately read-only/non-mutating (mirrors lock.py status) -- physical pruning happens on register/unregister writes, which is sufficient because session_holders() always filters by expires_at at read time regardless of what is still on disk, so correctness for check_lock.py's actual security-relevant path never depends on list's side effects. (2) blackboard.py claim and lock.py acquire were NOT touched and do not call session.py -- verified by inspection, registration stays an explicit, separate coordinator act. (3) No existing harness_common.py function signature was changed; only one new path constant (SESSION_HOLDERS) and one new function (session_holders()) were added.

VERIFIED (replayed, fresh holder probe-ver, me forced to 'main' via env -u). AC1 registered->exit0/unregistered->exit2: SAME lock holder probe-ver flips exit0 (registered) -> exit2 (after unregister) with me=main, isolating session_holders() as cause; cross-engine gemini-runner (unregistered)->exit2. AC2 register/unregister/list+TTL: all subcommands work; probe-ttl --ttl 3 in list immediately, gone after 4s (read-time filter: still on disk=True), unregister physically prunes (on disk=False). AC3 fail-open+compile: empty stdin->0, malformed json->0, py_compile all .harness/bin OK. AC4 README: lines 68-79 document mechanism + 'registered = this session coordinated agents ONLY' + 'blackboard.py claim and lock.py acquire never auto-register'. No-auto-register: grep session_holders/SESSION_HOLDERS in blackboard.py+lock.py = none (only an argparse help string 'register artifact'); final registry list = ONLY substrate-worker-1/-2, no harness-verifier despite my T-007/T-008 claims. Static: check_lock.py:42 'holder==me or holder in hc.session_holders()' fail-open via try/except; session.py list read-only. ANOMALY (non-blocking, pre-existing, candidate future G-finding): AC2's literal '--ttl 1' is timing-flaky - now_iso truncates to whole seconds while now_utc has microseconds, so a 1s window races truncation+fork (producer saw gone, I saw live); orthogonal harness_common quirk, NOT a T-008 defect; auto-expiry proven robustly at --ttl 3.
